data_out.description: Filtered scan index data frame (CSV or R object) containing only PRM scans with columns: scan, scanType, rtmin, rtmax, and computed delta (inter-scan interval)
Summary statistics report documenting total PRM scan count, delta distribution (mean, min, max), and boolean pass/fail for 22-scan-cycle consistency

domain_knowledge: PRM (parallel reaction monitoring) is a targeted MS/MS acquisition mode where the instrument cycles through a fixed set of precursor m/z values; a consistent inter-scan delta indicates stable, repeatable cycle timing.

The rawrr::readIndex() function returns a data frame where scanType encodes both the MS level and the scan filter string; filtering requires matching the exact filter pattern assigned during instrument method configuration.

A 22-scan delta in a PRM experiment typically represents one complete cycle of N targeted precursors acquired in sequence; deviation from this value signals acquisition irregularities or method configuration issues.
The example file was acquired on a Thermo Q Exactive HF using HCD fragmentation at normalized collision energy (NCE) of 27 with centroiding and lock mass correction applied during acquisition.

direct_checks: file_exists: verify that 20181113_010_autoQC01.raw is accessible via ExperimentHub accession EH4547 or MassIVE dataset MSV000086542
script_runs: verify that rawrr::readIndex(f) executes without error on the sample file and returns a data frame with at least columns 'scan', 'MSOrder', and 'scanType'
field_present: verify that the returned index data frame contains a 'scanType' or equivalent column that identifies PRM scan events
output_matches_reference: for all consecutive pairs of PRM scans in the index, verify that the difference in scan number is exactly 22 (byte-for-byte exact match to reported cycle length)
row_count_equals: robust to parameter choices — count the number of PRM scan events and confirm it is a positive integer consistent with a multi-cycle targeted experiment
expert_review: Verify that the filter string or scanType identifier used to isolate PRM events from the index correctly distinguishes parallel reaction monitoring scans from other MS/MS modes (e.g., DDA, full scan)
Confirm that a delta of 22 scans between consecutive PRM events is consistent with the documented instrument configuration and expected cycle structure for the autoQC01 experiment
Assess whether any missing or irregular scan numbers (gaps > 22, or non-uniform spacing) warrant investigation into data quality or scan type classification errors

evidence_snippets.text: [results] Does the parallel reaction monitoring (PRM) acquisition in the example raw file maintain consistent scan spacing across all cycles targeting the LGGNEQVTR++ precursor ion?: 'When subsetting it for our scan type of interest it becomes clear that the example data was recorded using parallel reaction monitoring (PRM) since the parent ion 487.2567 was isolated in'

[results] The delta between consecutive PRM scans targeting LGGNEQVTR++ (487.2567) is consistently 22 scans, representing one complete PRM cycle.: 'The delta between consecutive scans is always 22 scans (one PRM cycle) and the `depedencyType` of the MS2 scans is `NA` (since it was triggered by an inclusion list).'

[methods] 20181113_010_autoQC01.raw file from MassIVE dataset MSV000086542: 'The example file `20181113_010_autoQC01.raw` used throughout this manuscript contains Fourier-transformed Orbitrap spectra (FTMS) recorded on a Thermo Fisher Scientific Q Exactive HF'

[results] Filtered scan index data frame (CSV or R object) containing only PRM scans with columns: scan, scanType, rtmin, rtmax, and computed delta (inter-scan interval): 'rawrr::readIndex(rawfile = rawfile) |> subset(scanType == ...)'

[results] Summary statistics report documenting total PRM scan count, delta distribution (mean, min, max), and boolean pass/fail for 22-scan-cycle consistency: 'rawrr::readIndex(rawfile = rawfile) |> subset(scanType == ...)'

[methods] RawFileReader: 'Specifically, `R` functions requesting access to data stored in binary raw files (reader family functions listed in Table 1) invoke compiled `C#` wrapper methods using a system call'

[discussion] The specific scan filter string or column value that uniquely identifies PRM scan events within the readIndex output is not stated.: 'Filter scans by type 'FTMS + c NSI Full ms2 487.2567@hcd27.00 [100.0000-1015.0000]''

[discussion] The expected cycle length of 22 scans for PRM experiments on this instrument or in this dataset is not explicitly documented in the provided text.: 'No direct statement in provided text; sub-task scope cites '22 scans (one full cycle)' as a target but this is not verified against any reported figure or table in the section text.'

cmd: library(rawrr); idx <- readIndex('{inputs.raw_file}'); prm_idx <- idx[grepl('PRM', idx$scanType, ignore.case=TRUE), ]; prm_idx$delta <- c(NA, diff(prm_idx$scan)); summary_stats <- data.frame(total_prm_scans=nrow(prm_idx), mean_delta=mean(prm_idx$delta, na.rm=TRUE), min_delta=min(prm_idx$delta, na.rm=TRUE), max_delta=max(prm_idx$delta, na.rm=TRUE), pass_22_cycle=all(prm_idx$delta == 22, na.rm=TRUE)); write.csv(prm_idx[, c('scan', 'scanType', 'rtmin', 'rtmax', 'delta')], 'prm_scans_filtered.csv', row.names=FALSE); write.csv(summary_stats, 'prm_validation_report.csv', row.names=FALSE); cat('\n=== PRM Delta Validation Report ===\n'); print(summary_stats)
executable.grounding_notes: Unverified by the executable guard — command runs inline code (-c/-e); its API calls cannot be validated against package files. Treat this command as a best-effort inference; verify the entry point and APIs against the real code before running.

expected_outputs: Filtered scan index data frame (CSV or R object) containing only PRM scans with columns: scan, scanType, rtmin, rtmax, and computed delta (inter-scan interval)
Summary statistics report documenting total PRM scan count, delta distribution (mean, min, max), and boolean pass/fail for 22-scan-cycle consistency
finding: The delta between consecutive PRM scans targeting LGGNEQVTR++ (487.2567) is consistently 22 scans, representing one complete PRM cycle.
methodology_summary: Install and initialize the rawrr package with compiled C# wrapper methods bound to the RawFileReader .NET assembly.
Invoke rawrr::readIndex() to extract the complete scan metadata index from the binary .raw file via system call to the managed C# layer.
Parse the scanType field to identify and filter rows matching the PRM acquisition pattern (fixed precursor m/z and HCD collision energy).
Compute inter-scan deltas as the difference between consecutive scan numbers in the filtered PRM subset.
Validate that all deltas equal 22 scans, confirming single-cycle consistency across the acquisition run.
Validation: Pass criterion is 100% of observed inter-scan deltas equal 22 scans; any deviation flags acquisition irregularity.
missing_information: The specific scan filter string or column value that uniquely identifies PRM scan events within the readIndex output is not stated.
The expected cycle length of 22 scans for PRM experiments on this instrument or in this dataset is not explicitly documented in the provided text.

task_description: Load the scan index from 20181113_010_autoQC01.raw using rawrr::readIndex(), filter for PRM (parallel reaction monitoring) scan events, and validate that the delta between consecutive PRM scans is consistently 22 scans per acquisition cycle.
task_objective: Load the scan index from 20181113_010_autoQC01.raw using rawrr::readIndex(), filter for the 'FTMS + c NSI Full ms2 487.2567@hcd27.00 [100.0000-1015.0000]' scan type, and verify consistent 22-scan spacing between consecutive PRM events.

task_outputs.label: Filtered scan index with PRM scans and inter-scan deltas

title: Reproduce the PRM cycle delta of 22 scans using rawrr::readIndex on the autoQC01 raw file
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Filtered scan index data frame (CSV or R object) containing only PRM scans with columns: scan, scanType, rtmin, rtmax, and computed delta (inter-scan interval), Summary statistics report documenting total PRM scan count, delta distribution (mean, min, max), and boolean pass/fail for 22-scan-cycle consistency.
workflow_description: 1. Install rawrr runtime executable and load the rawrr R package. 2. Call rawrr::readIndex() on the 20181113_010_autoQC01.raw file to retrieve the complete scan index table. 3. Filter the index data frame to retain only rows where scanType matches the PRM acquisition method pattern (e.g., 'FTMS + c NSI Full ms2' with a fixed precursor m/z and HCD energy). 4. Extract the scan numbers from the filtered rows and compute the difference between consecutive scan indices. 5. Validate that all inter-scan deltas equal 22 scans, confirming one complete MS/MS cycle between consecutive targeted acquisitions. 6. Generate a summary report documenting the number of PRM scans detected, the observed delta values, and pass/fail status of the 22-scan-cycle criterion.
